title: [InstCombine] Add one-use check when folding fabs over selects

tests.source_program:
declare void @usef128(fp128)

declare fp128 @llvm.fabs.f128(fp128)

define fp128 @test_fabs_select_quad_precision_multiuse_constants(i1 %cond) {
  %sel = select i1 %cond, fp128 0xL00000000000000000000000000000000, fp128 0xL3FFF0000000000000000000000000000
  call void @usef128(fp128 %sel)
  %fabs = call fp128 @llvm.fabs.f128(fp128 %sel)
  ret fp128 %fabs
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare void @usef128(fp128)

declare fp128 @llvm.fabs.f128(fp128)

define fp128 @test_fabs_select_quad_precision_multiuse_constants(i1 %cond) {
  %sel = select i1 %cond, fp128 0xL00000000000000000000000000000000, fp128 0xL3FFF0000000000000000000000000000
  call void @usef128(fp128 %sel)
  %fabs = call fp128 @llvm.fabs.f128(fp128 %sel)
  ret fp128 %fabs
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only demonstrates f32/f64 types. Quad precision (f128/fp128) is a distinct floating point type not mentioned. Additionally, this tests multi-use with BOTH constants (optimization applies) which is mentioned, but the f128 type itself is outside scope.

declare void @usef128(fp128)

declare fp128 @llvm.fabs.f128(fp128)

define fp128 @test_fabs_select_quad_precision_multiuse_constants(i1 %cond) {
  %sel = select i1 %cond, fp128 0xL00000000000000000000000000000000, fp128 0xL3FFF0000000000000000000000000000
  call void @usef128(fp128 %sel)
  %fabs = call fp128 @llvm.fabs.f128(fp128 %sel)
  ret fp128 %fabs
}

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes the 'either operand is constant' condition for single-use selects but does not explicitly cover the case where BOTH operands are constants, which is also optimized and can fold further.

#0:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, float 1.000000, float -2.000000
  %fabs = fabs float %sel
  ret float %fabs
}
=>
define float @test_fabs_select_single_use_both_constants(i1 %cond) {
#0:
  %fabs = select i1 %cond, float 1.000000, float 2.000000
  ret float %fabs
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes the 'at least one constant' condition for single-use selects but does not explicitly document the case where BOTH operands are constants. This is a stricter subcase that still optimizes but isn't highlighted in the summary.
